title: Venice 3-Day Romantic Getaway
metaTitle: Venice 3-Day Romantic Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Venice, Italy: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including St. Mark's Basilica (Pala d'Oro & Treasury) and Doge's Palace. Prices from $1121 per person (estimate).
highlights: Sunset gondola ride through quiet back canals
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Venice, Italy — Humid subtropical climate with cool, misty winters (highs 8°C, occasional acqua alta flooding) and warm, humid summers (highs 28°C). April to June and September to October bring mild, pleasant weather while avoiding the worst summer crowds and heat. This itinerary blends St. Mark's Basilica (Pala d'Oro & Treasury), Doge's Palace, Rialto Bridge with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including St. Mark's Basilica (Pala d'Oro & Treasury), Doge's Palace, Rialto Bridge
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Venice
itinerary.description: Land at Venice Marco Polo Airport (VCE) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to St. Mark's Basilica (Pala d'Oro & Treasury) — Byzantine-domed cathedral with golden mosaics. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Venice: Doge's Palace & St. Mark's Campanile
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Doge's Palace — Gothic palace and seat of Venetian government. In the afternoon, continue to St. Mark's Campanile — Bell tower with panoramic lagoon views. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Gallerie dell'Accademia or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to VCE for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book Doge's Palace and St. Mark's Basilica tickets online to skip long lines.
Get lost intentionally in quieter Cannaregio and Dorsoduro to escape San Marco crowds.
Check the tide forecast in autumn/winter — acqua alta can flood St. Mark's Square.
Eat where locals eat: bacari (wine bars) serving cicchetti are cheaper and more authentic than sit-down tourist restaurants.
Venice charges a day-tripper access fee on certain peak dates — check requirements before visiting.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Waterproof shoes for possible acqua alta flooding (autumn/winter)
A refillable water bottle for public fountains
Comfortable shoes for extensive bridge-and-alley walking
thingsToDo: Ponte dei Sospiri (Bridge of Sighs) exterior
thingsToAvoid: Unlicensed gondoliers or "helpers" who approach with unsolicited offers — book through official stands.
Feeding pigeons in St. Mark's Square — it's prohibited and fined.
Buying "designer" bags from street vendors — counterfeits carry legal risk for buyers too.
Restaurants with tourist-menu photos directly off St. Mark's Square — overpriced and low quality.
Wheeled suitcases over Venice's many small bridges — consider a backpack instead.
topRestaurants: Cantina Do Spade — Cicchetti & Wine Bar ($$)
hiddenGems: Squero di San Trovaso gondola workshop
Isola di San Michele cemetery island
familyActivities: Lagoon boat tour to Burano and Torcello
luxuryExperiences: Michelin dining at a canal-view restaurant
Private water taxi tours of the lagoon islands
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset gondola ride through quiet back canals
Candlelit dinner overlooking the Grand Canal
Private Murano glassblowing experience for two
Champagne toast in St. Mark's Square
budgetExperiences: Free wander through Cannaregio and Dorsoduro
Vaporetto ride along the Grand Canal as a budget "cruise"
Cicchetti and wine crawl at neighborhood bacari
Free views from the Rialto Bridge and Zattere

festivalCalendar.name: Venice Biennale
festivalCalendar.timing: May to November, odd/even years alternating art/architecture

localEtiquette: Dress modestly (covered shoulders/knees) when entering St. Mark's Basilica.
Avoid sitting on church steps or bridges to eat — some areas fine for littering/loitering.
Address gondoliers and boat staff politely; haggling over quoted gondola prices is unusual.
Keep to the right when walking in narrow calli (alleys) to let foot traffic flow.
A day-tripper tourist tax applies for some visitors — check current entry requirements before arrival.
localTransportGuide: Venice has no cars — get around on foot or via the ACTV vaporetto (water bus) network, with multi-day passes offering good value for island hopping. Water taxis are fast but expensive; gondolas are for scenic leisure rather than practical transport.
bestTimeToVisit: April to June and September to October bring mild, pleasant weather while avoiding the worst summer crowds and heat.
weather: Humid subtropical climate with cool, misty winters (highs 8°C, occasional acqua alta flooding) and warm, humid summers (highs 28°C).
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~9.8h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Venice?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Venice?
faqs.answer: April to June and September to October bring mild, pleasant weather while avoiding the worst summer crowds and heat. Humid subtropical climate with cool, misty winters (highs 8°C, occasional acqua alta flooding) and warm, humid summers (highs 28°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Venice safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Venice is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Venice?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Venice is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset gondola ride through quiet back canals experience.

reviews.author: Elena Cohen
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Zattere waterfront promenade and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Lukas Kowalski
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Venice is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially St. Mark's Square (Piazza San Marco). The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Ethan Novak
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Murano glass-blowing demonstration and Dorsoduro district. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Lucas Costa
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Ponte dei Sospiri (Bridge of Sighs) exterior and Scuola Grande di San Rocco. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Tom Costa
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Gallerie dell'Accademia and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Chen Costa
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Cannaregio neighborhood in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.
